summary: Administrative cadastre from the Gaffron collection (likely Pachacamac) — a combined land survey and governance register dated September 1461 CE. 97 cords, 5 colors. Unlike UR1095 (pure land survey), UR1087 adds governance vocabulary: chapa (guardian), pina (who?), kata (conceal), wata (year). OKR confirms TEXT khipu ('no numerical interpretation possible'). First word kata (conceal) = thesis of an inspection report about fraud/hidden assets. Also discussed by Nordenskiold (1925). Companion of UR1095 and AS090 in the Gaffron collection. 31 STRING, 77% coverage.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  97 cords (L1: 89, L2: 8). Architecture: 0-SEUL, 2 clusters (1 header + 1 main block of 87 cords).
  5 colors: W(56), AB(23), VB(8), DB(6), BG(4).
  Provenance: 'Peru' — Gaffron collection = likely Pachacamac.
  Museum: Museum Five Continents, Munich (formerly Volkerkunde).
  Associated: AS087-094 all Gaffron. Includes AS090 (formal register) and UR1095 (cadastral survey).
  OKR: 'No numerical interpretation is possible as the knot clusters do not conform to the base ten positional system. Nineteen different knots...' = TEXT khipu confirmed.
  OKR: 'Long knots on this sample are Z or (in a few cases) no axis knots.' Z-direction dominant.
  31 STRING (77% hits — 24/31), 65 INT, 1 EMPTY.
  Date: September 1461 CE (C3 val=23 = year offset from 1438, C4 kata val=9 = month 9). HIGH confidence, confirmed by independent session.

FIRST WORD = THESIS
  Cord 4 (AB): kata = 'to conceal' — frames the entire register as an inspection report about fraud/hidden assets.

LAST WORD = CONCLUSION
  Cord 87 (W): chapi = 'thorn' — geographic landmark. Confirms CADASTRAL, not juridical: a juridical khipu ends on a verdict, not a plant.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR1087 is an administrative cadastre — a combined land survey and governance register. Unlike UR1095 (a pure land survey with siqa/qama/qata), UR1087 adds governance vocabulary: chapa (guardian/sentinel), pina (who? = questioning responsibility), kata (conceal), wata (year). Records not just WHAT the terrain looks like but WHO is responsible for it, WHEN, and whether anything has been hidden.

KEY VOCABULARY
  Cadastral/geographic core: qaqa (rock x1), taqa (boundary x2), paqa (dawn/east x1), chaqa (gorge/bridge x1), kaqa (wall-AY x1).
  Administrative overlay: kata (conceal x1), chapa (guardian x1), pina (who? x1), wata (year x1), tiki (all/each x1), waki (cooperation x2), kaki (corn-AY x1), siki (base x1), chapi (thorn x1), piki (flea x3).
  Decomposed forms: siqapa = siqa+pa (of the ramp) = pure cadastral grammar. piqati = piqa+ti (who are all [responsible]?) = administrative interrogative.
  Proper name: Chikima (cord 25) — base kikima not in dictionary.

COLOR-AS-ROLE
  W (56 cords, 18 STRING): GEOGRAPHIC DATA — terrain description layer.
  AB (23 cords, 8 STRING): SUBJECT/IDENTITY — who occupies/guards the terrain.
  VB (8 cords, 2 STRING): SPECIAL CATEGORY — taqa (boundary).
  DB (6 cords, 3 STRING): OBJECTS/TEMPORAL — wata(year) on DB confirms it encodes WHEN.
  BG (4 cords, 1 STRING): IDENTITY — tiki (all/each).
  AB = 24% (vs 6% in UR1095) confirms stronger identity/governance component.

STRUCTURE
  Section 1 (1 cord): Header — single value [2].
  Section 2 (87 cords): Main block — entire register in one continuous section.
    Cords 1-18: OPENING: kata, tiqa, taqa, qaqa, piki...
    Cords 19-43: MIDDLE: taqa, wata+piki, siki, piqati, pipi, pina, chikima...
    Cords 44-87: CLOSING: paqa, waki, siqapa, chaqa, kaki, chapa, kaqa, kusi, chapi.
  No section breaks — one continuous register.

DATING
  Document date: September 1461 CE (C3=23 + C4=9, confirmed independently).
  Dual-channel encoding: kata reads as 'conceal' (text) while its turn-value reads as month 9 (number).
  DB color = temporal column: wata (year) on cord 49 is DB color. All 6 DB cords form a temporal column.
  Internal chronology: ~1450 (earliest reference) -> ~1458 (boundary established) -> ~1460 (concealment discovered) -> Sept 1461 (document written).

CROSS-CORPUS — THE GAFFRON COLLECTION TRIO
  UR1087 vs UR1095 vs AS090:
    UR1087: Admin cadastre, 97 cords, 5 colors, 77% hits
    UR1095: Land survey, 212 cords, 4 colors, 88% hits
    AS090: Formal register, 58 cords, 1 color (monochrome W), 83% hits
  5 shared cadastral terms: qaqa, taqa, kaqa, paqa, chaqa.
  UR1087 = UR1095 + governance layer. If UR1095 is the 'site plan' (where things ARE), UR1087 is the 'management plan' (who GUARDS what, and what's been CONCEALED).

NARRATIVE
  A cadastral inspector walks the Pachacamac precinct. He notes the terrain: rocks (qaqa), boundaries (taqa x2), a gorge or bridge (chaqa), walls (kaqa), a ramp (siqapa = 'of the ramp'), and the eastern orientation (paqa). But unlike UR1095's pure survey, this inspector also documents the ADMINISTRATION: who guards (chapa) each sector, who is responsible (pina = 'for whom?'), whether anything has been concealed (kata), and when the inspection took place (wata = year).
  The name Chikima appears among the interrogatives — possibly the person questioned about responsibility. The Aymara words (kaki = corn, kaqa = wall) confirm coastal Aymara linguistic zone.
  This is the MANAGEMENT document that goes with UR1095's SURVEY document. One describes the terrain; the other tracks who is in charge.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Administrative cadastre analysis, dating September 1461 CE
OKR: 'No numerical interpretation possible' — TEXT khipu confirmed
Nordenskiold, E. (1925). The Secret of the Peruvian Quipus, Table 3
Gaffron collection: UR1095 (cadastral survey), AS090 (formal register), all Munich
Cross-corpus: UR1095 (siqa x5 = pyramids with ramps), UR203 (ceque registry, Pachacamac)
